course_info.title: 3D Game Development
course_info.meeting_schedule: Monday, Wednesday, Friday - 3 hours per week

learning_objectives: Understand the scope and complexity of 3D game development
Identify key differences between 2D and 3D game development

lecture_notes.introduction: Welcome to 3D Game Development. This course will take you from 2D concepts into the complex world of three-dimensional interactive experiences.
lecture_notes.demonstration: Live demo of Unity 3D interface and basic scene setup
setup_requirements: Visual Studio or VS Code configured

review.value: Right-handed vs left-handed coordinate systems, world vs local space
Vertex processing, rasterization, fragment shading, post-processing

lecture_notes.introduction: Mathematical foundations are crucial for 3D graphics programming. Today we'll cover the essential math concepts.
lecture_notes.demonstration: Interactive demonstration of transformations using Unity's transform component

review.value: 4x4 matrix combining translation, rotation, and scale
4-component representation of rotation that avoids gimbal lock

learning_objectives: Understand the complete graphics rendering pipeline

lecture_notes.introduction: The graphics pipeline is the heart of 3D rendering. Understanding each stage is essential for advanced graphics programming.
lecture_notes.demonstration: Writing a simple vertex/fragment shader pair in Unity

review.value: Processes individual vertices, handles transformations and lighting calculations
Determines final pixel color, handles texturing and complex lighting

lecture_notes.introduction: Physics simulation brings realism to 3D games. We'll explore both the theory and practical implementation.
lecture_notes.demonstration: Setting up physics bodies and collision detection in Unity
setup_requirements: Basic understanding of calculus and physics

review.value: Broad phase and narrow phase detection, spatial partitioning techniques
Euler integration vs. Verlet integration for stability

questions.question: What is the result of multiplying a 4x4 transformation matrix by a position vector (2, 3, 4, 1) if the matrix represents a translation of (1, 0, 0)?
Explain the primary advantage of using quaternions over Euler angles for 3D rotations.
questions.answer: Quaternions avoid gimbal lock, provide smooth interpolation, and are more efficient for combining rotations.

questions.question: List the main stages of the graphics rendering pipeline in order.
questions.answer: Vertex processing, primitive assembly, rasterization, fragment processing, per-fragment operations

questions.question: Write a simple HLSL vertex shader function that transforms a vertex position from object space to clip space.

major_assignments.title: 3D Scene Construction and Basic Interaction
major_assignments.description: Create a fully interactive 3D scene with multiple objects, materials, lighting, and basic player controls. This assignment focuses on fundamental 3D development skills.
deliverables: Unity project files with complete scene

major_assignments.title: Advanced Shader Programming
major_assignments.description: Develop custom shaders for various visual effects including lighting models, post-processing effects, and animated materials.
objectives: Master vertex and fragment shader programming
Implement advanced lighting and material effects
Create visually impressive and optimized shaders
deliverables: Collection of custom shaders with documentation

weekly_quizzes.description: Regular assessment of theoretical knowledge and practical understanding

lab_assignments.description: Hands-on programming exercises and technical skill development

major_assignments.description: Comprehensive projects demonstrating mastery of key concepts

midterm_examination.description: Comprehensive assessment of first half of course material

final_project.description: Capstone project demonstrating integration of all course concepts

course_policies.attendance: Attendance is mandatory for all lab sessions and project presentations. More than 2 unexcused absences may result in course failure.
course_policies.late_submissions: Late assignments will be penalized 10% per day. No submissions accepted more than 5 days late without prior arrangement.
course_policies.collaboration: Individual assignments must be completed independently. Group projects require equal contribution from all members with peer evaluation.
course_policies.academic_integrity: All code must be original or properly attributed. Use of AI tools must be disclosed and approved. Plagiarism will result in course failure.
course_policies.technology_requirements: Students must have access to a computer capable of running Unity 3D and modern development tools. Lab computers are available but personal machines are recommended.
